zotero.abstractNote: Normalizing flows are a powerful tool for density estimation and generative modeling. However, they are typically designed to model the joint distribution of a set of variables without regard for the underlying causal structure. In this work, we introduce Causal Normalizing Flows (CNFs), a framework that allows for the integration of causal knowledge into normalizing flows. We provide a theoretical characterization of the conditions under which a flow can be considered causal, and we propose a practical implementation that leverages these conditions to learn causal models from observational data. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a variety of synthetic and real-world datasets, showing that CNFs can recover the underlying causal structure and perform accurate interventional inference.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems

extra.Editors: A. Oh and T. Neumann and A. Globerson and K. Saenko and M. Hardt and S. Levine

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 36 (NeurIPS 2023)
